question:
5. Evaluate in closed form
\[
\sum_{k=1}^{n}\binom{n}{k} k^{2}
\]
(page 560)

Note:
\[
\binom{n}{k}=\frac{n(n-1) \cdots(n-k+1)}{1 \cdot 2 \cdots k}
\]
solution:
First Solution.
\[
\begin{aligned}
\sum_{k=1}^{n}\binom{n}{k} k^{2}= & \sum_{k=1}^{n} \frac{n!}{(n-k)!k!}[k(k-1)+k] \\
= & \sum_{k=2}^{n} \frac{n(n-1)(n-2)!}{(n-k)!(k-2)!}+\sum_{k=1}^{n} \frac{n(n-1)!}{(n-k)!(k-1)!} \\
& =n(n-1)^{\prime \prime} \sum_{0}^{2}\binom{n-2}{j}+n \sum_{i-1}^{\prime \prime \Sigma^{\prime}}\binom{n-1}{j} \\
& =n(n-1) 2^{\prime \prime} 2^{2}+n 2^{\prime \prime} \quad 1=n(n+1) 2^{\prime \prime 2} .
\end{aligned}
\]

Second Solution. If we apply the operator \( x(d / d x) \) to
\[
(1+x)^{n}=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k} x^{k},
\]
we obtain the identity
\[
n x(1+x)^{n-1}=\sum_{k=1}^{n}\binom{n}{k} k x^{k} .
\]

Applying it again, we find
\[
n(n-1) x^{2}(1+x)^{n-2}+n x(1+x)^{n-1}=\sum_{k=1}^{n}\binom{n}{k} k^{2} x^{k}
\]

Put \( x=1 \) and we have
\[
\sum_{k=1}^{n}\binom{n}{k} k^{2}=n(n-1) 2^{n-2}+n 2^{n-1}=n(n+1) 2^{n-2} .
\]

descriptive_long.question:
5. Evaluate in closed form
\[
\sum_{indexk=1}^{paramn}\binom{paramn}{indexk} indexk^{2}
\]
(page 560)

Note:
\[
\binom{paramn}{indexk}=\frac{paramn(paramn-1) \cdots(paramn-indexk+1)}{1 \cdot 2 \cdots indexk}
\]
descriptive_long.solution:
First Solution.
\[
\begin{aligned}
\sum_{indexk=1}^{paramn}\binom{paramn}{indexk} indexk^{2}= & \sum_{indexk=1}^{paramn} \frac{paramn!}{(paramn-indexk)!indexk!}[indexk(indexk-1)+indexk] \\
= & \sum_{indexk=2}^{paramn} \frac{paramn(paramn-1)(paramn-2)!}{(paramn-indexk)!(indexk-2)!}+\sum_{indexk=1}^{paramn} \frac{paramn(paramn-1)!}{(paramn-indexk)!(indexk-1)!} \\
& =paramn(paramn-1)^{\prime \prime} \sum_{0}^{2}\binom{paramn-2}{indexj}+paramn \sum_{indexi-1}^{\prime \prime \Sigma^{\prime}}\binom{paramn-1}{indexj} \\
& =paramn(paramn-1) 2^{\prime \prime} 2^{2}+paramn 2^{\prime \prime} \quad 1=paramn(paramn+1) 2^{\prime \prime 2} .
\end{aligned}
\]

Second Solution. If we apply the operator \( \variablex(d / d \variablex) \) to
\[
(1+\variablex)^{paramn}=\sum_{indexk=0}^{paramn}\binom{paramn}{indexk} \variablex^{indexk},
\]
we obtain the identity
\[
paramn \variablex(1+\variablex)^{paramn-1}=\sum_{indexk=1}^{paramn}\binom{paramn}{indexk} indexk \variablex^{indexk} .
\]

Applying it again, we find
\[
paramn(paramn-1) \variablex^{2}(1+\variablex)^{paramn-2}+paramn \variablex(1+\variablex)^{paramn-1}=\sum_{indexk=1}^{paramn}\binom{paramn}{indexk} indexk^{2} \variablex^{indexk}
\]

Put \( \variablex=1 \) and we have
\[
\sum_{indexk=1}^{paramn}\binom{paramn}{indexk} indexk^{2}=paramn(paramn-1) 2^{paramn-2}+paramn 2^{paramn-1}=paramn(paramn+1) 2^{paramn-2} .
\]

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
5. Evaluate in closed form
\[
\sum_{sandstone=1}^{windswept}\binom{windswept}{sandstone} sandstone^{2}
\]
(page 560)

Note:
\[
\binom{windswept}{sandstone}=\frac{windswept(windswept-1) \cdots(windswept-sandstone+1)}{1 \cdot 2 \cdots sandstone}
\]
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
First Solution.
\[
\begin{aligned}
\sum_{sandstone=1}^{windswept}\binom{windswept}{sandstone} sandstone^{2}= & \sum_{sandstone=1}^{windswept} \frac{windswept!}{(windswept-sandstone)!sandstone!}[sandstone(sandstone-1)+sandstone] \\
= & \sum_{sandstone=2}^{windswept} \frac{windswept(windswept-1)(windswept-2)!}{(windswept-sandstone)!(sandstone-2)!}+\sum_{sandstone=1}^{windswept} \frac{windswept(windswept-1)!}{(windswept-sandstone)!(sandstone-1)!} \\
& =windswept(windswept-1)^{\prime \prime} \sum_{0}^{2}\binom{windswept-2}{footprint}+windswept \sum_{lightning-1}^{\prime \prime \Sigma^{\prime}}\binom{windswept-1}{footprint} \\
& =windswept(windswept-1) 2^{\prime \prime} 2^{2}+windswept 2^{\prime \prime} \quad 1=windswept(windswept+1) 2^{\prime \prime 2} .
\end{aligned}
\]

Second Solution. If we apply the operator \( blueberry(d / d blueberry) \) to
\[
(1+blueberry)^{windswept}=\sum_{sandstone=0}^{windswept}\binom{windswept}{sandstone} blueberry^{sandstone},
\]
we obtain the identity
\[
windswept \, blueberry(1+blueberry)^{windswept-1}=\sum_{sandstone=1}^{windswept}\binom{windswept}{sandstone} sandstone \, blueberry^{sandstone} .
\]

Applying it again, we find
\[
windswept(windswept-1) \, blueberry^{2}(1+blueberry)^{windswept-2}+windswept \, blueberry(1+blueberry)^{windswept-1}=\sum_{sandstone=1}^{windswept}\binom{windswept}{sandstone} sandstone^{2} blueberry^{sandstone}
\]

Put \( blueberry=1 \) and we have
\[
\sum_{sandstone=1}^{windswept}\binom{windswept}{sandstone} sandstone^{2}=windswept(windswept-1) 2^{windswept-2}+windswept 2^{windswept-1}=windswept(windswept+1) 2^{windswept-2} .
\]

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Problem:
<<<
5. Evaluate in closed form
\[
\sum_{staticindex=1}^{unboundedsize}\binom{unboundedsize}{staticindex} staticindex^{2}
\]
(page 560)

Note:
\[
\binom{unboundedsize}{staticindex}=\frac{unboundedsize(unboundedsize-1) \cdots(unboundedsize-staticindex+1)}{1 \cdot 2 \cdots staticindex}
\]
>>>
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
First Solution.
\[
\begin{aligned}
\sum_{staticindex=1}^{unboundedsize}\binom{unboundedsize}{staticindex} staticindex^{2}= & \sum_{staticindex=1}^{unboundedsize} \frac{unboundedsize!}{(unboundedsize-staticindex)!staticindex!}[staticindex(staticindex-1)+staticindex] \\
= & \sum_{staticindex=2}^{unboundedsize} \frac{unboundedsize(unboundedsize-1)(unboundedsize-2)!}{(unboundedsize-staticindex)!(staticindex-2)!}+\sum_{staticindex=1}^{unboundedsize} \frac{unboundedsize(unboundedsize-1)!}{(unboundedsize-staticindex)!(staticindex-1)!} \\
& =unboundedsize(unboundedsize-1)^{\prime \prime} \sum_{0}^{2}\binom{unboundedsize-2}{stillcounter}+unboundedsize \sum_{fixedunit-1}^{\prime \prime \Sigma^{\prime}}\binom{unboundedsize-1}{stillcounter} \\
& =unboundedsize(unboundedsize-1) 2^{\prime \prime} 2^{2}+unboundedsize 2^{\prime \prime} \quad 1=unboundedsize(unboundedsize+1) 2^{\prime \prime 2} .
\end{aligned}
\]

Second Solution. If we apply the operator \( verticalaxis(d / d verticalaxis) \) to
\[
(1+verticalaxis)^{unboundedsize}=\sum_{staticindex=0}^{unboundedsize}\binom{unboundedsize}{staticindex} verticalaxis^{staticindex},
\]
we obtain the identity
\[
unboundedsize verticalaxis(1+verticalaxis)^{unboundedsize-1}=\sum_{staticindex=1}^{unboundedsize}\binom{unboundedsize}{staticindex} staticindex verticalaxis^{staticindex} .
\]

Applying it again, we find
\[
unboundedsize(unboundedsize-1) verticalaxis^{2}(1+verticalaxis)^{unboundedsize-2}+unboundedsize verticalaxis(1+verticalaxis)^{unboundedsize-1}=\sum_{staticindex=1}^{unboundedsize}\binom{unboundedsize}{staticindex} staticindex^{2} verticalaxis^{staticindex}
\]

Put \( verticalaxis=1 \) and we have
\[
\sum_{staticindex=1}^{unboundedsize}\binom{unboundedsize}{staticindex} staticindex^{2}=unboundedsize(unboundedsize-1) 2^{unboundedsize-2}+unboundedsize 2^{unboundedsize-1}=unboundedsize(unboundedsize+1) 2^{unboundedsize-2} .
\]

garbled_string.question:
5. Evaluate in closed form
\[
\sum_{zuwyqnpl=1}^{yzvxqbel}\binom{yzvxqbel}{zuwyqnpl} zuwyqnpl^{2}
\]
(page 560)

Note:
\[
\binom{yzvxqbel}{zuwyqnpl}=\frac{yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel-1) \cdots(yzvxqbel-zuwyqnpl+1)}{1 \cdot 2 \cdots zuwyqnpl}
\]
garbled_string.solution:
First Solution.
\[
\begin{aligned}
\sum_{zuwyqnpl=1}^{yzvxqbel}\binom{yzvxqbel}{zuwyqnpl} zuwyqnpl^{2}= & \sum_{zuwyqnpl=1}^{yzvxqbel} \frac{yzvxqbel!}{(yzvxqbel-zuwyqnpl)!zuwyqnpl!}[zuwyqnpl(zuwyqnpl-1)+zuwyqnpl] \\
= & \sum_{zuwyqnpl=2}^{yzvxqbel} \frac{yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel-1)(yzvxqbel-2)!}{(yzvxqbel-zuwyqnpl)!(zuwyqnpl-2)!}+\sum_{zuwyqnpl=1}^{yzvxqbel} \frac{yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel-1)!}{(yzvxqbel-zuwyqnpl)!(zuwyqnpl-1)!} \\
& =yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel-1)^{\prime \prime} \sum_{0}^{2}\binom{yzvxqbel-2}{vbtrsqmf}+yzvxqbel \sum_{lkjmdghr-1}^{\prime \prime \Sigma^{\prime}}\binom{yzvxqbel-1}{vbtrsqmf} \\
& =yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel-1) 2^{\prime \prime} 2^{2}+yzvxqbel 2^{\prime \prime} \quad 1=yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel+1) 2^{\prime \prime 2} .
\end{aligned}
\]

Second Solution. If we apply the operator \( pqowmczn(d / d pqowmczn) \) to
\[
(1+pqowmczn)^{yzvxqbel}=\sum_{zuwyqnpl=0}^{yzvxqbel}\binom{yzvxqbel}{zuwyqnpl} pqowmczn^{zuwyqnpl},
\]
we obtain the identity
\[
yzvxqbel pqowmczn(1+pqowmczn)^{yzvxqbel-1}=\sum_{zuwyqnpl=1}^{yzvxqbel}\binom{yzvxqbel}{zuwyqnpl} zuwyqnpl pqowmczn^{zuwyqnpl} .
\]

Applying it again, we find
\[
yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel-1) pqowmczn^{2}(1+pqowmczn)^{yzvxqbel-2}+yzvxqbel pqowmczn(1+pqowmczn)^{yzvxqbel-1}=\sum_{zuwyqnpl=1}^{yzvxqbel}\binom{yzvxqbel}{zuwyqnpl} zuwyqnpl^{2} pqowmczn^{zuwyqnpl}
\]

Put \( pqowmczn=1 \) and we have
\[
\sum_{zuwyqnpl=1}^{yzvxqbel}\binom{yzvxqbel}{zuwyqnpl} zuwyqnpl^{2}=yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel-1) 2^{yzvxqbel-2}+yzvxqbel 2^{yzvxqbel-1}=yzvxqbel(yzvxqbel+1) 2^{yzvxqbel-2} .
\]

kernel_variant.question:
For every positive integer n determine a closed-form expression for  
\[
T_n=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}\,k^{4}\,2^{\,k}\,3^{\,n-k}.
\]
kernel_variant.solution:
1.  A suitable generating function  
    \[
    F(t):=(3+2t)^{n}=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}(2t)^{k}
            =\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\,t^{k}
    \]
    carries at \(t^{k}\) exactly the summand \(\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\).

2.  The r-th derivative satisfies  
    \[
      F^{(r)}(t)=\sum_{k=r}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\,k^{\underline{r}}\,t^{\,k-r},
    \]
    whence at \(t=1\)
    \[
      F^{(r)}(1)=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\,k^{\underline{r}},
      \qquad k^{\underline{r}}:=k(k-1)\cdots(k-r+1).
    \]

3.  Explicit derivatives of \(F(t)=(3+2t)^{n}\):
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
      F'(t)   &= 2n(3+2t)^{n-1}, \\
      F''(t)  &= 4n(n-1)(3+2t)^{n-2},\\
      F'''(t) &= 8n(n-1)(n-2)(3+2t)^{n-3},\\
      F''''(t)&=16n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)(3+2t)^{n-4}.
    \end{aligned}
    \]
    Evaluated at \(t=1\):
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
      F'(1)   &= 2n\;5^{\,n-1},\\
      F''(1)  &= 4n(n-1)\;5^{\,n-2},\\
      F'''(1) &= 8n(n-1)(n-2)\;5^{\,n-3},\\
      F''''(1)&=16n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)\;5^{\,n-4}.
    \end{aligned}
    \]

4.  Expressing an ordinary power through falling factorials
    \[
      k^{4}=k^{\underline{4}}+6k^{\underline{3}}+7k^{\underline{2}}+k^{\underline{1}},
    \]
    we convert the required sum to
    \[
      T_n=F''''(1)+6F'''(1)+7F''(1)+F'(1).
    \]

5.  Insert the values from step 3:
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
      T_n={}&16n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)5^{n-4}
            +6\cdot8n(n-1)(n-2)5^{n-3}\\
           &+7\cdot4n(n-1)5^{n-2}
            +2n5^{n-1}.
    \end{aligned}
    \]

6.  Factor \(2n5^{\,n-4}\) and simplify:
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
       T_n
       &=2n5^{\,n-4}\Bigl[8(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)
                         +120(n-1)(n-2)
                         +350(n-1)
                         +125\Bigr] \\
       &=2n5^{\,n-4}\bigl(8n^{3}+72n^{2}+78n-33\bigr).
    \end{aligned}
    \]

Hence the closed form is  
\[
\boxed{\,T_n = 2n\,(8n^{3}+72n^{2}+78n-33)\;5^{\,n-4}\,}.
\]

metadata.difficulty_analysis:
•  Higher-order moment: the exponent of k is raised from 2 (original) and 3 (kernel) to 4, forcing manipulation of fourth-order falling factorials.  
•  Mixed bases: the weight \(2^{k}3^{\,n-k}\) destroys the usual symmetry and demands a two-parameter generating function; simple substitutions \(x=1\) no longer work.  
•  Advanced tools: the solution relies on (i) generating functions, (ii) repeated differentiation, (iii) conversion between ordinary and falling powers, and (iv) heavy polynomial algebra.  
•  Volume of work: four derivatives must be computed and combined, versus at most two in the original.  
These additions collectively make the enhanced variant significantly more intricate and conceptually demanding than both the original problem and the current kernel version.

original_kernel_variant.question:
For every positive integer n determine a closed-form expression for  
\[
T_n=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}\,k^{4}\,2^{\,k}\,3^{\,n-k}.
\]
original_kernel_variant.solution:
1.  A suitable generating function  
    \[
    F(t):=(3+2t)^{n}=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}(2t)^{k}
            =\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\,t^{k}
    \]
    carries at \(t^{k}\) exactly the summand \(\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\).

2.  The r-th derivative satisfies  
    \[
      F^{(r)}(t)=\sum_{k=r}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\,k^{\underline{r}}\,t^{\,k-r},
    \]
    whence at \(t=1\)
    \[
      F^{(r)}(1)=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}3^{\,n-k}2^{\,k}\,k^{\underline{r}},
      \qquad k^{\underline{r}}:=k(k-1)\cdots(k-r+1).
    \]

3.  Explicit derivatives of \(F(t)=(3+2t)^{n}\):
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
      F'(t)   &= 2n(3+2t)^{n-1}, \\
      F''(t)  &= 4n(n-1)(3+2t)^{n-2},\\
      F'''(t) &= 8n(n-1)(n-2)(3+2t)^{n-3},\\
      F''''(t)&=16n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)(3+2t)^{n-4}.
    \end{aligned}
    \]
    Evaluated at \(t=1\):
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
      F'(1)   &= 2n\;5^{\,n-1},\\
      F''(1)  &= 4n(n-1)\;5^{\,n-2},\\
      F'''(1) &= 8n(n-1)(n-2)\;5^{\,n-3},\\
      F''''(1)&=16n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)\;5^{\,n-4}.
    \end{aligned}
    \]

4.  Expressing an ordinary power through falling factorials
    \[
      k^{4}=k^{\underline{4}}+6k^{\underline{3}}+7k^{\underline{2}}+k^{\underline{1}},
    \]
    we convert the required sum to
    \[
      T_n=F''''(1)+6F'''(1)+7F''(1)+F'(1).
    \]

5.  Insert the values from step 3:
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
      T_n={}&16n(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)5^{n-4}
            +6\cdot8n(n-1)(n-2)5^{n-3}\\
           &+7\cdot4n(n-1)5^{n-2}
            +2n5^{n-1}.
    \end{aligned}
    \]

6.  Factor \(2n5^{\,n-4}\) and simplify:
    \[
    \begin{aligned}
       T_n
       &=2n5^{\,n-4}\Bigl[8(n-1)(n-2)(n-3)
                         +120(n-1)(n-2)
                         +350(n-1)
                         +125\Bigr] \\
       &=2n5^{\,n-4}\bigl(8n^{3}+72n^{2}+78n-33\bigr).
    \end{aligned}
    \]

Hence the closed form is  
\[
\boxed{\,T_n = 2n\,(8n^{3}+72n^{2}+78n-33)\;5^{\,n-4}\,}.
\]
